title: SCL Joined Lexicon — NRC VAD + OPP + NMA
subtitle: 57K terms: NRC VAD v2.1 + SCL-OPP + SCL-NMA full outer join
description:
Full outer join of three NRC affective lexicons on `term`, with a unified valence hierarchy (OPP > NMA > VAD) and phrase composition analysis.

## Lexicons joined

| Lexicon | Terms | Scale | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **NRC VAD v2.1** | 54,801 | bipolar −1 to +1 | Unigrams, bigrams, 2 trigrams. MWE-first lookup. |
| **SCL-OPP** | 1,178 | bipolar −1 to +1 | Opposing-polarity phrases (e.g. `happy accident`, `bad luck`) |
| **SCL-NMA** | 3,207 | bipolar −1 to +1 | Negator/modal/adverb phrases (e.g. `not bad`, `barely good`) |

All three lexicons use Best-Worst Scaling and are directly comparable on the same bipolar [-1, +1] scale.

## Valence hierarchy

```
valence_source precedence: opp > nma > vad
```

When a term appears in multiple lexicons, OPP takes precedence (most semantically specific), then NMA, then VAD. The `valence` column always reflects the highest-priority source.

## Sentiment compositionality

The core insight: **the distance between two models of meaning**.

- **Selected valence** (`valence`) — human-annotated phrase score from the lexicon
- **Computed valence** (`computed_valence`) — naive word-averaging (token mean)
- **Composition shift** = `valence − computed_valence`

Positive shift → phrase is more positive than its parts predict
Negative shift → phrase is more negative than its parts predict (negation, irony)

Three detection methods are pre-computed:

| Column | Method | Threshold |
|--------|--------|-----------|
| `abs_composition_shift` | Absolute — `|shift|` | ≥ 0.30 strong, ≥ 0.50 very strong |
| `shift_zscore` | Z-score — statistical significance | `|z| ≥ 2` flags outliers |
| `shift_percentile` | Percentile rank of `|shift|` (0–100) | top 5% / 1% = tail |

**`composition_type`** classifies the linguistic mechanism:

| Type | Mechanism | Example |
|------|-----------|---------|
| `negation` | Negator token reverses polarity | `not love` (shift −1.75) |
| `degree_modifier` | Intensifier/diminisher adverb | `pretty bad`, `very very bad` |
| `modal` | Modal verb introduces conditionality | `might succeed` |
| `opposing_polarity` | OPP phrase — tokens predict wrong polarity | `happy accident`, `guilty pleasure` |
| `idiomatic` | VAD multi-word, `|shift| ≥ 0.30`, no explicit modifier | `zero tolerance`, `sex toy` |
| `nma_unclassified` | NMA phrase without detected modifier | unigram NMA entries |
| `direct` | Unigram or minimal shift | single words |

Mean shift by type: negation 0.59 → idiomatic 0.46 → degree_modifier 0.29 → opposing_polarity 0.19

**Notable examples:**
- `not love` → shift −1.747 (negation, largest negative)
- `zero tolerance` → shift −1.438 (idiomatic, VAD)
- `no harm` → shift +1.143 (negation, largest positive)

Population: 56,900 terms with a shift. Mean −0.027, std 0.152.

## Columns (28 total)

| Column | Description |
|---|---|
| `term` | Lexicon term (lowercased phrase) |
| `ngrams` | Word count (1 = unigram, 2 = bigram, 3 = trigram) |
| `in_vad` | True if term appears in NRC VAD v2.1 |
| `in_opp` | True if term appears in SCL-OPP |
| `in_nma` | True if term appears in SCL-NMA |
| `vad_valence` | NRC VAD v2.1 valence, bipolar −1 to +1 |
| `vad_arousal` | NRC VAD v2.1 arousal, bipolar −1 to +1 |
| `vad_dominance` | NRC VAD v2.1 dominance, bipolar −1 to +1 |
| `opp_valence` | SCL-OPP valence for opposing-polarity phrases |
| `opp_pos` | SCL-OPP POS pattern (e.g. `A+N`, `N+N`) |
| `opp_freq` | SCL-OPP tweet frequency (corpus count) |
| `opp_is_hashtag` | True if original SCL-OPP term was a hashtag (# removed) |
| `nma_valence` | SCL-NMA valence for negator/modal/adverb phrases |
| `valence_count` | Number of lexicons with a valence score (0–3) |
| `valence_variance` | Variance across lexicon scores (null if < 2 sources — use as confidence signal) |
| `valence_source` | Winning source: `opp` / `nma` / `vad` |
| `valence` | **Best available valence** from hierarchy (bipolar −1 to +1) |
| `negators_contained` | Negator tokens found within this term (word-boundary match) |
| `modals_contained` | Modal tokens found within this term |
| `adverbs_contained` | Intensifier/diminisher adverbs found within this term |
| `computed_valence` | Token-mean of hierarchy-selected unigram valence scores |
| `composition_shift` | `valence − computed_valence` — semantic nonlinearity |
| `abs_composition_shift` | `|composition_shift|` — magnitude, sign-agnostic |
| `shift_zscore` | Z-score of `composition_shift` over all terms with a shift |
| `shift_percentile` | Percentile rank (0–100) of `abs_composition_shift` |
| `shift_strong` | True if `abs_composition_shift ≥ 0.30` |
| `shift_very_strong` | True if `abs_composition_shift ≥ 0.50` |
| `composition_type` | Linguistic mechanism: `negation` / `degree_modifier` / `modal` / `opposing_polarity` / `idiomatic` / `nma_unclassified` / `direct` |
## Coverage

| Lexicon | Terms | % of 57K joined |
|---|---|---|
| NRC VAD v2.1 | 54,801 | 96.1% |
| SCL-OPP | 1,178 | 2.1% |
| SCL-NMA | 3,207 | 5.6% |
| Cross-validated (≥2 sources) | 1,983 | 3.5% |

## Data provenance

NRC Lexicons: [saifmohammad.com/WebPages/lexicons.html](https://saifmohammad.com/WebPages/lexicons.html)

SCL Lexicons: [saifmohammad.com/WebPages/SCL.html](https://www.saifmohammad.com/WebPages/SCL.html)

## Related datasets

- [TMDB Keyword Lexicon](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/bdelanghe/tmdb-keyword-lexicon) — 67,916 TMDB keywords scored using this joined lexicon
- [TMDB Movie VAD + Emotion Scores](https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/bdelanghe/tmdb-movie-vad-emotion-scores) — movie-level aggregation

**Source pipeline:** [github.com/bdelanghe/imdb-kaggle](https://github.com/bdelanghe/imdb-kaggle)

resources.description: 57,004 unique terms from full outer join of NRC VAD v2.1 + SCL-OPP + SCL-NMA with valence hierarchy and composition shift analysis.

fields.name: term
fields.description: Lexicon term (lowercased phrase)

fields.name: ngrams
fields.description: Word count: 1=unigram, 2=bigram, 3=trigram

fields.name: in_vad
fields.description: True if term appears in NRC VAD v2.1

fields.name: in_opp
fields.description: True if term appears in SCL-OPP (Opposing Polarity Phrases)

fields.name: in_nma
fields.description: True if term appears in SCL-NMA (Negator/Modal/Adverb phrases)

fields.name: vad_valence
fields.description: NRC VAD v2.1 valence, bipolar −1 (negative) to +1 (positive)

fields.name: vad_arousal
fields.description: NRC VAD v2.1 arousal, bipolar −1 (calm) to +1 (excited)

fields.name: vad_dominance
fields.description: NRC VAD v2.1 dominance, bipolar −1 (submissive) to +1 (dominant)

fields.name: opp_valence
fields.description: SCL-OPP valence for opposing-polarity phrases (bipolar −1 to +1)

fields.name: opp_pos
fields.description: SCL-OPP POS pattern of the phrase (e.g. A+N for adjective+noun)

fields.name: opp_freq
fields.description: SCL-OPP tweet frequency — how often this phrase appeared in the corpus

fields.name: opp_is_hashtag
fields.description: True if the original SCL-OPP entry was a hashtag (# prefix removed during load)

fields.name: nma_valence
fields.description: SCL-NMA valence for negator/modal/adverb phrases (bipolar −1 to +1)

fields.name: valence_count
fields.description: Number of lexicons (0–3) with a valence score. Higher = more confident.

fields.name: valence_variance
fields.description: Variance across lexicon valence scores. Non-null only when valence_count >= 2. High variance = lexicons disagree.

fields.name: valence_source
fields.description: Source of the winning valence: opp (SCL-OPP), nma (SCL-NMA), vad (NRC VAD)

fields.name: valence
fields.description: Best available bipolar valence [−1, +1] following OPP > NMA > VAD hierarchy

fields.name: negators_contained
fields.description: Negator tokens found within this term using word-boundary match (e.g. 'not', 'no', 'never', 'cannot')

fields.name: modals_contained
fields.description: Modal tokens found within this term (e.g. 'can', 'could', 'should', 'might')

fields.name: adverbs_contained
fields.description: Intensifier/diminisher adverbs found within this term (e.g. 'very', 'barely', 'extremely')

fields.name: computed_valence
fields.description: Token-mean of hierarchy-selected unigram valence scores. For unigrams: equals valence (shift=0). For multi-word: naive additive model.

fields.name: composition_shift
fields.description: valence − computed_valence. Positive = phrase more positive than parts predict. Negative = more negative (negation, irony). Only meaningful for multi-word terms.

fields.name: abs_composition_shift
fields.description: |composition_shift|. Magnitude of semantic nonlinearity regardless of direction.

fields.name: shift_zscore
fields.description: Z-score of composition_shift: (shift − mean_shift) / std_shift over all terms with a shift. |z| ≥ 2 = statistically significant outlier.

fields.name: shift_percentile
fields.description: Percentile rank (0–100) of abs_composition_shift. Top 5% = strong shift; top 1% = extreme shift.

fields.name: shift_strong
fields.description: True if abs_composition_shift ≥ 0.30. Heuristic threshold for a meaningful composition effect.

fields.name: shift_very_strong
fields.description: True if abs_composition_shift ≥ 0.50. Heuristic threshold for a very strong composition effect.

fields.name: composition_type
fields.description: Linguistic mechanism: negation (negator token), degree_modifier (adverb), modal (modal verb), opposing_polarity (OPP phrase), idiomatic (VAD multi-word |shift|≥0.30), nma_unclassified (NMA without detected modifier), direct (minimal shift)
